mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based demand destruction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening, a contraction in industrial production signals easing supply bottlenecks and lower inflation expectations, which reduces discount rate pressure and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles or fails to produce a positive mean return difference in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5%, market participants anticipate aggressive policy normalization, causing immediate liquidity withdrawal and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of observations above the threshold or the crossing event shows no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market shift, which depresses consumer spending forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in >65% of instances or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in <50% of high-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sharp drop in industrial production signals severe supply constraints that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered observations or the signal loses predictive power when inflation falls below the threshold.
